content_of_post_to_comment:
After wasting years stuck in this useless doomscrolling trap, I finally decided to force myself to walk to unlock my apps. What came next was just brutal.

Days 1-7: Constant irritation. My brain was fried without that instant feed refresh, my legs hated being forced outside just to check TikTok, and I snapped at everyone over nothing. It felt like actual withdrawals.

Days 8-30: The flatline. 0 energy, feeling down, and having to fake a smile through the day while the urge to just lay in bed and swipe hit me constantly.

Days 31-90: The bargaining phase. For two whole months, my brain kept trying to convince me to just delete the step-tracker so I could have one quick peek at my feed. Not giving in to that and actually lacing up my shoes to earn my screen time was the real key to making it.

Day 100: Things finally clicked. The brain fog is completely gone. I’ve got my focus back.. I'm hitting PRs at the gym surprisingly (probably from all the extra daily steps), and actually making real connections with people again instead of staring at a screen. Life feels way better.

But here’s the reality check. Complacency is what gets you. The massive, intense cravings to sit and rot in front of an endless feed from the first few weeks are gone, but now there's just a quiet whisper telling me I'm cured and can just bypass the step-lock for today. Don't fall for it.

If you want to make it this far, willpower isn't enough. You have to tie your screen time to physical movement. I highly recommend using blocking apps that lock your doomscrolling behind a mandatory walk and literally do not give you an option to cancel or bypass it until you hit your steps. Close every single loophole. I think it's really the only way to beat it.

I'm 100 days in. Who's joining? Drop your day count below or hit me with :D if you're starting TODAY. No excuses and thanks for reading G
comment: That quiet "I'm cured" voice is way more dangerous than the early cravings because it sounds reasonable. Tying the habit to something you can't fake, like actually getting outside, turns it from relying on motivation into relying on friction. Curious if you've noticed the step requirement feeling automatic yet or if you still negotiate with yourself some days.
